layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Express.js routes and handlers for spatial queries, routing proxy, and search.

layers.name: Middleware Layer
layers.description: Request validation, authentication, and error handling middleware.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core backend logic and database connectivity.

layers.name: UI Frontend
layers.description: Client-side map interface and layer management.

layers.name: Data Pipeline
layers.description: Scripts for fetching, parsing, and transforming regional health/education data.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: GeoSphere WB+ is a regional map platform for West Bengal. Start with the README to understand the project architecture, including integration with PostGIS, OSRM, and Nominatim.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: The server entry point initializes the Express application, sets up middleware (compression, CORS, logging), and mounts the API routes. It is the core of the Node.js backend.

tour.title: Data Connectivity
tour.description: The db module manages the connection to the PostgreSQL/PostGIS database, facilitating spatial queries that power the platform's map features.

tour.title: Search & Geocoding
tour.description: These routes handle spatial search queries, leveraging the database to find locations and institutions across West Bengal.

tour.title: Hospital & School Pipeline
tour.description: The data pipeline captures regional data (like WBBSE schools and AISHE colleges) and transforms it into the PostGIS schema for mapping.

tour.title: Institutions Schema
tour.description: This SQL schema defines the tables for educational and health institutions, ensuring high-fidelity spatial data storage.

tour.title: Map Frontend
tour.description: The frontend implementation uses a map-based UI to visualize spatial layers and interact with the backend API endpoints.

tour.title: System Orchestration
tour.description: The start_all script automates the launch of all services, including the API, OSRM routing, and Nominatim geocoding.
